items.title: US Stocks: S&P, Nasdaq end lower as investors weigh Middle East conflict outlook
items.summary: U.S. stocks closed mostly lower as President Trump's conflicting messages on Iran and escalating Middle East conflict weighed on markets. Despite optimism over discussions, threats to the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian oil facilities fueled investor concerns. Fed Chair Powell's comments on inflation offered some support, while financial stocks rose on new 401(k) guidelines.

items.title: RBI defers implementation of capital market exposures norms to July 1
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has postponed new acquisition finance rules. The implementation is now set for July 1, 2026. This decision follows feedback from stakeholders. The central bank also updated its capital market exposures framework. These changes aim to clarify lending for mergers and acquisitions. The RBI is refining guidelines for domestic lenders.

items.title: Goldman Sachs picks stakes in Jio Financial, BHEL via block deals
items.summary: Jio Financial Services and BHEL saw significant block deals today as global financial giants traded shares. Morgan Stanley offloaded stakes in both companies, with Goldman Sachs acquiring positions. Other block deals involved BNP Paribas transacting across Siemens Energy India, GMR Airports, LG Electronics India, and Max Healthcare Institute.

items.title: XED, India's first GIFT city IPO, withdraws issue after weak demand
items.summary: XED Executive Development, the first company ​from India's low-tax ​GIFT City to launch an initial ​public offering, has withdrawn the IPO after delays with customer verification and weak market sentiments due to ‌the U.S.-Israeli ⁠war ⁠on Iran, it said on Monday.

items.title: BNP Paribas buys stakes in 4 stocks via block deals; LG Electronics, Max Health & 2 more
items.summary: BNP Paribas executed block deals in Siemens Energy India, GMR Airports, LG Electronics India and Max Healthcare. Despite institutional buying, all stocks declined, reflecting broader market weakness, profit booking and cautious sentiment across equities during the trading session.

items.title: Indian gas maker INOX Air Products plans $1 billion IPO, appoints bankers, sources say
items.summary: Indian industrial and medical gas maker ​INOX Air Products is planning ​to launch a $1 billion initial public offering in ​Mumbai, and has appointed Kotak, JPMorgan and Citi to manage the IPO, three sources familiar with the matter said.

items.title: ‘Five Nights At Freddy’s 2’ Coming To Peacock This Week
items.summary: "Five Nights at Freddy’s 2," the horror hit starring Josh Hutcherson and Matthew Lillard, is making its streaming video on demand debut on Peacock this week. Find out when you can watch the movie on the platform.

items.title: Major Businesses Still Increasing Prices Because Of Trump’s Tariffs, Survey Finds
items.summary: More than half of businesses said they plan to increase prices by at least 15% over the next six months, though it remains unclear how that could be impacted by the Supreme Court’s tariff ruling.

items.title: What Must Tottenham’s Next Manager Fix To Keep Them Up?
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur needs another new manager after firing Igor Tudor after only 44 days in the position of head coach.

items.title: White House Says Russian Oil Tanker Allowed To Cuba For ‘Humanitarian Reasons’
items.summary: Trump told reporters Sunday he has “no problem” with Cuba receiving a Russian oil tanker as the U.S. oil blockade has prompted a humanitarian crisis on the island.

items.title: Roberto De Zerbi Can Only Dream Of A ‘Spursy’ Tottenham Hotspur
items.summary: For De Zerbi, the best hope is to restore Tottenham to the days when ‘Spursy’ meant losing a Champions League final, not fighting relegation.​

items.title: 2026 Frozen Four Field Is Set After Dramatic NCAA Tournament Weekend
items.summary: The 16-team field in the 2026 NCAA men's hockey tournament has been whittled down to four. Here are the winners, losers, surprises and new pros that have emerged so far.

items.title: Army Launches Investigation Into Helicopter Flyby At Kid Rock’s Nashville Home
items.summary: Kid Rock posted videos over the weekend showing what appeared to be two AH-64 Apache helicopters hovering close to his home in Nashville.

items.title: ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Beats Another One Of Rosé’s Billboard Records
items.summary: KPop Demon Hunters sees "Golden" return to the top 10 on Billboard's Streaming Songs chart following its Academy Awards win.

items.title: The Mysterious Figure Mohamed Salah Says ‘Wanted Him Blamed’ Has Won
items.summary: Unless Salah reveals more, his departure stands as a symbolic indictment of Liverpool’s inability to manage its stars with the clarity that once defined its strategy.
